elapsed.description: Wall-clock time the estimator actually spent, in milliseconds.

timeout.description: Total timeout granted to the estimator, in milliseconds.

priceEstimate.description:
JSON envelope wrapping every event published to the bus. Consumers can
rely on `version` to evolve their parsers, on `timestamp` for ordering,
and on `requestId` to correlate events emitted while serving a single
inbound request (e.g. all the price estimates and the resulting quote of
one quote request share the same `requestId`).

timestamp.description:
RFC3339 timestamp (millisecond precision, UTC) of when the event was
published.

priceEstimate.title: Envelope

QuoteComputedEvent.description:
Emitted once the orderbook has finished computing a quote ("finished
computing quote"). Its main job is correlation: the envelope's `requestId`
ties it back to the [`crate::QuoteRequestedEvent`] and the per-estimator
[`crate::PriceEstimateEvent`]s of the same request, while `quoteId` links
forward to the resulting order via the `quotes` table.

appCode.description: `appCode` from the order's app-data document, if present.

quoteId.description:
Database id of the stored quote. Absent for `fast` quotes, which are
not persisted.

verified.description: Whether the quote was verified by simulation.

quoteComputed.description:
JSON envelope wrapping every event published to the bus. Consumers can
rely on `version` to evolve their parsers, on `timestamp` for ordering,
and on `requestId` to correlate events emitted while serving a single
inbound request (e.g. all the price estimates and the resulting quote of
one quote request share the same `requestId`).

timestamp.description:
RFC3339 timestamp (millisecond precision, UTC) of when the event was
published.

quoteComputed.title: Envelope

QuoteRequestedEvent.description:
Emitted for a validated quote request, just before price estimation runs.
Carries the request context that partner and quote analysis care about —
notably the `appCode` and the token symbols — which are not present on the
per-estimator [`crate::PriceEstimateEvent`].

appCode.description: `appCode` from the order's app-data document, if present.

buyTokenSymbol.description: Symbol of the buy token, if known.

sellTokenSymbol.description: Symbol of the sell token, if known.

quoteRequested.description:
JSON envelope wrapping every event published to the bus. Consumers can
rely on `version` to evolve their parsers, on `timestamp` for ordering,
and on `requestId` to correlate events emitted while serving a single
inbound request (e.g. all the price estimates and the resulting quote of
one quote request share the same `requestId`).

timestamp.description:
RFC3339 timestamp (millisecond precision, UTC) of when the event was
published.

quoteRequested.title: Envelope

WinningPriceEstimateEvent.description:
Emitted once price-estimate competition has picked the winning estimate that
becomes the official quote. Its job is correlation: among the
[`crate::PriceEstimateEvent`]s emitted for the same request (one per
competing estimator, all sharing the envelope `requestId`), the winning one
is the one whose `estimator` matches this event.

Exactly one is emitted per quote competition.

estimator.description: Name of the estimator whose price estimate won the competition.
